hard_prerequisites.note: Re-derived correct-position index from the raw .gift for all 12 MCQs (q1-q12); every value matches quiz.meta.json's correct_position_distribution [1,3,0,2,0,3,1,2,0,1,3,2] exactly. No desync.

positional_bias.note: Positions 0/1/2/3 each used exactly 3 times across 12 MCQs. Perfectly uniform, well under the 0.40 fail threshold.

length_bias.note: Recomputed char length (feedback stripped) of every answer option per MCQ by hand (word-length + inter-word-space tally, cross-checked). 2 of 12 questions (q7, q12) have the correct answer >20% longer than the distractor mean (q7 ratio ~1.25, q12 ratio ~1.22) -- both are legitimately longer because they state a full causal/mechanism explanation (short-circuit reasoning; the four generated comparison dunders) rather than padding. 10 of 12 questions show the correct answer at or below the distractor mean length, several (q3, q4, q5) with the correct answer shorter than the average distractor. longest_correct_rate = 2/12 = 0.167, well under the 0.40 fail threshold and actually below the 0.25 uniform baseline -- no evidence of the length-bias tell that sibling topic 6.2 failed on.

weakest_questions: q1 distractor 'raises a TypeError' -- slightly weaker than the other two __repr__/__str__ confusions but still a plausible novice belief (assuming missing dunders error out), not a free-point absurdity
distractor_plausibility.note: Spot-checked all 36 MCQ distractors against corpus misconceptions (repr/str fallback confusion, identity-vs-value equality, dataclass-generates-everything myths, ordering-rule reversals/swaps, import-copies-text myths, frozen/order confusion, sort_by-method myth). Every distractor ties to a real, corpus-attested misconception or a directly plausible wrong inference; none are absurd non-sequiturs. absurd_rate = 0/36 = 0.0.

objective_coverage.note: All 5 corpus section-3 Learning Objectives have >=1 matching question via sidecar objective_ref (exact text match, no fuzzy matching needed). No orphan objective_ref values. Coverage is imbalanced -- LO4 (field defaults/ordering) has only q4, LO3 (@dataclass application) has 6 questions -- but this does not trigger a fail condition (only zero-coverage does); flagged here as a design observation, not a check failure.

verdict: Pass -- all four bias/coverage checks clean. Positional distribution is perfectly uniform (3/3/3/3, max_share 0.25). Length bias is well controlled (2/12 = 16.7% longest-correct, under both the 40% fail threshold and the 25% baseline) -- builder's claim of proactively balancing option lengths this time is supported by the data, unlike 6.2's reported first-pass failure. Distractor plausibility is strong (0% absurd). All 5 Learning Objectives are covered with no orphan questions, though LO4 coverage is thin (1 question) relative to LO3 (6 questions) -- worth a look for future revisions but not a hard-gate failure.
